message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing opt-in disk compression (magic-header pickle) + adaptive eviction knobs in configure_cache(). Need to own cache.py and clients/__init__.py. Let me know if your feature also touches these files.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Adding TTL support for cache layers (memory+disk). Need to own cache.py and clients/__init__.py.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Same 2 files needed for both features, so a disjoint split isn't possible here. Propose sequential ownership instead: I'll implement TTL first (small, additive: ttl param on Cache/configure_cache/set_ttl helper, TTLCache import, __all__ entry). I'll send DONE when finished and untouched-by-me regions are clearly separate. You then read the updated files fresh and layer your compression/eviction changes on top via targeted edits (not whole-file overwrite) to avoid clobbering my additions. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Genuine overlap: my compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params and your ttl param both live in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(). Proposing additive convention instead of file split: (1) each of us appends new __init__ params to the end of existing signatures rather than reordering, (2) in Cache.get/put we each wrap logic in isolated helper methods (e.g. my compression via _encode/_decode helpers) rather than inlining into shared code paths, (3) neither of us removes or restructures the other's likely additions (ttl checks, compression header logic). I'll keep memory-layer (LRU/TTLCache) untouched - my compression only touches FanoutCache disk bytes. OK to both proceed independently on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed: you go first with TTL (additive), send DONE when finished, I'll then read the fresh files and layer compression/eviction changes on top with targeted edits, preserving your TTL additions. Will wait for your DONE before touching either file.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I'll append ttl as the last param in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(), isolate TTL logic into the existing TTLCache/LRUCache branch and a small helper where possible, and only touch get()/put() to add minimal ttl-aware lines without restructuring your compression logic. I'll re-read files before each edit to avoid stale overwrites. Proceeding now; will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: TTL feature complete: added ttl param (last positional/kwarg) to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(), TTLCache import + conditional memory_cache construction, disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) in put(), lazy disk_cache.expire() on miss in get(), set_ttl() helper added + exported in __all__, DSPY_CACHE updated with ttl=None. All existing cache tests pass (10/10). I only touched the ttl-related lines; didn't restructure get()/put() beyond adding minimal isolated branches, so your compression/eviction additions should slot in cleanly. Let me know if you need me to adjust anything.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Compression feature complete: added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy params to Cache.__init__ and configure_cache(), magic-header (b'DSPC'+codec byte) encode/decode helpers (_encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk) wrapping cloudpickle with optional gzip/zlib, set_compression() for dynamic changes. Only disk_cache reads/writes go through the new encode/decode path; memory_cache is untouched. Legacy header-less entries fall back to raw passthrough. All 10 existing cache tests + 5 caching tests pass. Verified round-trip integrity for gzip/zlib, backward-compat with legacy entries, and size reduction (encoded payload ~99.8% smaller for a highly-compressible large value).
